response.justification: Despite extensive full-stack and cross-platform experience, the candidate lacks demonstrable C++ expertise in industrial automation contexts, with no evidence of STL, multi-threading, message brokers, IEC 62443, or IoT protocols critical to the role; most C++ exposure is limited to academic or isolated projects (e.g., bachelor’s thesis), while professional work centers on JavaScript/TypeScript and DLang.
strengths: Strong full-stack and cross-platform development experience across web, embedded, and desktop systems.
Demonstrated leadership in open-source projects and technical mentoring via workshops and community contributions.
Proven ability to deliver scalable systems using event-driven architectures and cloud-native tools (Kubernetes, AWS CDK).
concerns: Minimal professional C++/STL experience; core role requires 8–12 years of hands-on C++ development.
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), MQTT, REST in automation contexts, or industrial protocols (OPC UA, Modbus).
Lacks documented experience with secure development lifecycle (SDL), UML architecture, or multi-threaded high-performance systems in production.
